channel.title: World
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from World, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: ICE to spend $38bn turning warehouses into detention centers, documents show
item.description: US homeland security eyeing 24 buildings, some as ‘primary locations’ for deportations, in escalation of Trump agenda US Citizenship and Immigration S...

item.title: Macron swipes at Trump tariffs and Greenland threats; Zelenskyy has strong words for Russia – as it happened
item.description: This live blog is now closed. If you need a primer on what’s on the agenda for the next three days, I spoke with the MSC’s head of policy Nicole Koeni...

item.title: Two Britons among three dead after avalanche in French Alps
item.description: A skier from France is also killed with manslaughter investigation to be carried out by mountain rescue police Two Britons are among three skiers to h...

item.title: US lawmakers ask Mandelson to testify to Congress over Epstein relationship
item.description: Letter from committee on oversight says it is clear the former ambassador ‘holds critical information’ Peter Mandelson has been asked to testify to th...

item.title: ICE plans to spend $38.3bn converting warehouses to detention centers, documents show, as DHS shutdown looms – US politics live
item.description: Centers would have capacity for tens of thousands of people to be held; talks over funding bill stall hours before shutdown The annual rate of US infl...

item.title: Federal officers may have lied about shooting of migrant in Minneapolis, officials say
item.description: ICE chief Todd Lyons says the two officers were immediately placed on administrative leave.

item.title: Don Lemon pleads not guilty in Minnesota church protest case
item.description: The former CNN host and eight others have been charged for interrupting a church service to protest immigration raids in the state.

item.title: Two Britons among three dead in French Alps avalanche
item.description: The Britons had been part of a group of five people accompanied by an instructor, skiing off-piste in Val d'Isère.

item.title: 'Intense, aggressive, entertaining' - what will Tudor bring to Spurs ?
item.description: After Igor Tudor agrees to take charge of Tottenham for the remainder of the season, BBC Sport takes a look at what supporters can expect to see from ...

item.title: Weston wins emphatic skeleton gold for first GB medal
item.description: Matt Weston cements his status as the best skeleton racer in the world as he makes history by winning Olympic gold in emphatic fashion in Cortina, sec...
